prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear! A CPU is like the brain of the computer—it’s the part that thinks and figures things out. When you click on a game or type a message, the CPU is the busy little worker inside making it all happen. It doesn’t store your pictures or songs—that’s what the memory and hard drive are for—but it tells everything else what to do and when. Even though I don’t quite get all the fancy bits and bytes, I know it’s mighty important, just like your brain helps you run and play!
